title: pisum sativum subsp. humile
intro: Pisum sativum subsp. humile, also known as the wild or garden pea, is a perennial legume that is closely related to the edible pea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. The health benefits of Pisum sativum subsp. humile include its ability to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, with some traditional preparations also including the leaves and seeds in capsule or powder form for medicinal use.
